summary: I was born in Somalia in 1994, but I left when I was very young. In 2012, after finishing high school, I decided to return there to reconnect with my father and see the country for myself. Despite the media's portrayal of Somalia as a place of war and chaos, I discovered both the struggles and the beauty of the country. That visit deeply changed my perspective and inspired me to want to help the people there in a meaningful way.

My family and I moved to Kenya in 1999 when I was about five years old. We didn't stay there long because we eventually migrated to Minnesota around 2000. Kenya was a brief stop in our journey from Somalia to the United States, but it was part of my early childhood experience before settling in America.

We first moved to Minnesota around 2000 when I was five years old, but we didn't stay long because we didn't have much family there. Later, after growing up in Seattle, I moved back to Minnesota in 2014 to pursue my studies to become a K-5 teacher. Minnesota has been both an early and later home for me during different stages of my life.

After leaving Minnesota, my family moved to Seattle where I grew up and went to school. It was in Seattle that I started my life and felt like a normal kid despite all the migrations. I finished high school there in 2012 before deciding to take a journey back to Somalia. Seattle was really where I spent my formative years.
